signals.signal: VISION.md explicitly excludes agent-hierarchy frameworks (manager-of-managers) as a default architecture
signals.detail: Will NOT merge: 'Agent-hierarchy frameworks (manager-of-managers / nested planner trees) as a default architecture'. This is a deliberate anti-pattern stance -- OpenClaw prefers flat agent dispatch over deep orchestration trees. Direct contrast to WorkTrain's coordinator pipeline model.

signals.signal: Plugin strategy: core stays lean, new capabilities go to ClawHub (external registry)
signals.detail: VISION: 'Core stays lean; optional capability should usually ship as plugins. We are generally slimming down core while expanding what plugins can do.' ClawHub is the plugin marketplace. This is an ecosystem moat play.

signals.signal: Memory: converging on one recommended default path (currently multiple memory options)
signals.detail: VISION: 'Today we ship multiple memory options; over time we plan to converge on one recommended default path.' Currently one memory plugin slot active at a time.

signals.signal: Governance: steipete-led, PSPDFKit connection, heavily individual-contributor model
signals.detail: Top contributor (steipete / Peter Steinberger) has 24k commits out of ~35k in 90 days -- extremely concentrated. Strong individual vision driver, not committee-driven. High bus factor, but also high velocity.

signals.signal: Sponsored by OpenAI, GitHub, NVIDIA, Vercel -- broadly supported across the AI ecosystem
signals.detail: Marquee sponsors listed in README. Signals ecosystem backing, not niche project.

signals.signal: Windows (WSL2) + Linux systemd support -- cross-platform operator reach
signals.detail: schtasks.ts for Windows task scheduler, systemd.ts for Linux. Explicit WSL2 recommendation in README. WorkTrain is macOS-only today.

signals.signal: LLM-assisted exec approvals (announced): use LLM reasoning for tool approval policy
signals.detail: Issue #79157: 'LLM-assisted exec approvals / policy reasoning'. Not yet shipped but filed as a feature, suggests direction toward runtime policy intelligence.
